fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the user's ID whose number originates from the User ID field of the Outlook Synch. User Setup table.

fields.name: ItemLedgerEntryNoLatest
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entity code the number of which originates from the Synch. Entity Code field of the Outlook Synch. User Setup table.

fields.name: ValueEntryNoLatest
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Outlook entity to use in the synchronization process. To fill in this field, the program copies the value of the Element No. field from the Outlook Synch. Entity Element table which corresponds to Synchronization Level 2.

fields.name: LastUpdated
fields.tooltip: Shows the name of the Outlook collection which is selected to be synchronized.

fields.name: CurrentInventoryValue
fields.tooltip: Specifies the current inventory value (in local currency), calculated as the sum of Cost Amount (Actual) + Cost Amount (Expected) on posted Value Entries for this item. Run the Adjust Cost - Item Entries batch job to ensure the amount is up to date.

fields.name: ExpiredStockValue
fields.tooltip: Specifies the inventory value (in local currency) of quantities whose expiration date is earlier than the work date. Calculated as the sum of Cost Amount (Actual) + Cost Amount (Expected) from Value Entries applied to open Item Ledger Entries (Remaining Qty. > 0) with Expiration Date < Work Date. Run the Adjust Cost - Item Entries batch job to ensure the value is up to date. Only meaningful for items where expiration/lot dates are tracked.

fields.name: SalesGrowthRateThisPeriod
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage change in sales compared to the previous period in the fiscal year, calculated as ((Sales in the current period in the fiscal year - Sales in the prior period in the fiscal year) ÷ Sales in the prior period in the fiscal year) x 100%. A positive value indicates growth, while a negative value indicates a decline in sales.

fields.name: NetSalesLCYThisPeriod
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total revenue (in local currency) from sales for the current period in fiscal year after deducting given discounts and returns. This value represents the actual income generated from sales transactions during the fiscal year. Calculated as: Net Sales = Total sales in the period  - Total returns - Total given discounts.

fields.name: GrossMarginThisPeriod
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of revenue remaining after deducting the cost of goods sold (COGS) for the current period in the fiscal year. This metric indicates how efficiently the company produces and sells its products. Calculated as: Gross Margin (%) = ((Net Sales - COGS) ÷ Net Sales) x 100%. A higher percentage reflects better profitability.

fields.name: ReturnRateThisPeriod
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of sold quantity that were returned during the current period in the fiscal year. This metric helps measure product quality and customer satisfaction. Calculated as: Return Rate (%) = (Returned Quantity ÷ Total Sold Quantity) x 100%. A lower percentage indicates fewer returns and higher product acceptance.

fields.name: SalesGrowthRateThisFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage change in sales compared to the previous fiscal year, calculated as ((Sales in current fiscal year - Sales in the last fiscal year) ÷ Sales in the last fiscal year) x 100%. A positive value indicates growth, while a negative value indicates a decline in sales.

fields.name: NetSalesLCYThisFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total revenue (in local currency) from sales for the current fiscal year after deducting given discounts and returns. This value represents the actual income generated from sales transactions during the fiscal year. Calculated as: Net Sales = Total sales in the fiscal year - Total returns in the fiscal year - Total given discounts in the fiscal year.

fields.name: GrossMarginThisFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of revenue remaining after deducting the cost of goods sold (COGS) for the current fiscal year. This metric indicates how efficiently the company produces and sells its products. Calculated as: Gross Margin (%) = ((Net Sales - COGS) ÷ Net Sales) x 100%. A higher percentage reflects better profitability.

fields.name: ReturnRateThisFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of sold quantity that were returned during the current fiscal year. This metric helps measure product quality and customer satisfaction. Calculated as: Return Rate (%) = (Returned Quantity ÷ Total Sold Quantity) x 100%. A lower percentage indicates fewer returns and higher product acceptance.

fields.name: SalesGrowthRateLastFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage change in sales compared to the previous fiscal year, calculated as ((Sales in the last fiscal year - Sales in the prior fiscal year) ÷ Sales in the prior fiscal year) x 100%. A positive value indicates growth, while a negative value indicates a decline in sales.

fields.name: NetSalesLCYLastFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total revenue (in local currency) from sales for the last fiscal year after deducting given discounts and returns. This value represents the actual income generated from sales transactions during the last fiscal year. Calculated as: Net Sales = Total sales in the last fiscal year - Total returns in the last fiscal year - Total given discounts in the last fiscal year.

fields.name: GrossMarginLastFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of revenue remaining after deducting the cost of goods sold (COGS) for the last fiscal year. This metric indicates how efficiently the company produces and sells its products. Calculated as: Gross Margin (%) = ((Net Sales - COGS) ÷ Net Sales) x 100%. A higher percentage reflects better profitability.

fields.name: ReturnRateLastFY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of sold quantity that were returned during the last fiscal year. This metric helps measure product quality and customer satisfaction. Calculated as: Return Rate (%) = (Returned Quantity ÷ Total Sold Quantity) x 100%. A lower percentage indicates fewer returns and higher product acceptance.

fields.name: NetSalesLCYLifetime
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total revenue (in local currency) from sales after deducting given discounts and returns. This value represents the actual income generated from sales transactions during the lifetime. Calculated as: Net Sales = Total sales so far - Total returns so far - Total given discounts so far.

fields.name: GrossMarginLifetime
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of revenue remaining after deducting the cost of goods sold (COGS) for the lifetime. This metric indicates how efficiently the company produces and sells its products. Calculated as: Gross Margin (%) = ((Net Sales - COGS) ÷ Net Sales) x 100%. A higher percentage reflects better profitability.

fields.name: ReturnRateLifetime
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of sold quantity that were returned during the lifetime. This metric helps measure product quality and customer satisfaction. Calculated as: Return Rate (%) = (Returned Quantity ÷ Total Sold Quantity) x 100%. A lower percentage indicates fewer returns and higher product acceptance.
